sentences.text: ASIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY POLICY AND PROCEDURE STATEMENT Reference AA Issued By PRESIDENT Section 4 Responsible Officer VP-AA Academic and Serial No.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: 1-1 Date Issued 10 February 2004 Administrative Affairs No.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: of Pages 4 Date Revised 18 March 2022 TITLE: ACADEMIC INTEGRITY IN RESEARCH AND PUBLICATION I.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: PURPOSE To set forth the policies and procedures for academic integrity in research and publication.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: DEFINITIONS Research is the creative expression of scholarship upon which the quality of teaching and viability of the Institute’s graduate programs hinge.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: There are basically three different types of research which are recognized and supported by the Institute: 1.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Independent research is based on the faculty’s interests and capabilities in the academic areas of the Institute.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: This is usually carried out in conjunction with student research.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Sponsored research is based on specific areas of interest of the Institute, mainly funded by sponsors, and supplemented by Institute subsidies.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Research may be sponsored by a government agency, private or non-government organization, foundation, or individual.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Sponsored research projects are based on problems relevant to the development of the region and to the education of AIT students who are being prepared to participate in the development of their own countries.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Such research projects involve both faculty and students, the participation of the latter to be as part of their master or doctoral degree requirements.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: The direct beneficiaries of research output are the profession, society or country.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Contracted research is based on the specific needs and interests of external clients who underwrite the cost of the research in return for access to the results.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: The external client expects to benefit from the research delivered to the client, usually in the form of reports, software or prototypes.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Contracted research may entail confidentiality and restriction on publication.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Research outputs invariably result in publications in journals, conference proceedings, books, and the like.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Publication helps disseminate the research results for furtherance of research endeavors, for scrutiny by peer groups, and for utilization of applicable information for public good.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: AA-4-1-1: ACADEMIC INTEGRITY IN RESEARCH AND PUBLICATION III.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: PUBLICATION AND AUTHORSHIP Publication record is perhaps the most important measure of performance of academic institutions.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: High quality publications are indispensable for promotion of faculty to higher academic ranks.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Authorship of academic papers is thus highly valued.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Authorship, however, confers responsibility and accountability of the information contained in the manuscript.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Authors of research papers, for example, implicitly endorse that the research was conducted as it is described.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Authors must be responsible for what has been written in return for the credit and recognition that authorship brings.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: It is to be expected that each author/co-author has: (a) made a significant contribution to the article design, data analysis and interpretation, and other scholarly effort, (b) participated in the article preparation, and (c) approved the manuscript.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: For any multi-authored article based on a student thesis, research study or dissertation, the student should normally be the first author, unless there is documented evidence that the journal required that the authorship should be written in a different order.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: However, the Chair of the student’s Program Committee should serve as the corresponding author with any journal.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: If the student is the sole author of an article based on his/her thesis, research study or dissertation, the student serves as the corresponding author with any journal.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Authorship is not sufficiently justifiable where participation is only through acquisition of funding, collection of data, general supervision of a research unit, or performance of normal duties of a research support staff.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Honorary or courtesy authorship is not acceptable.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: ACADEMIC MISCONDUCT All members of the AIT community are expected to comply with the relevant Institute policy and procedures in the conduct of research and to observe high standards of academic integrity and ethical behavior in publishing research outputs by self- regulation and by adherence to professional standards.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Serious deviations from commonly accepted practices in the academic community constitute academic misconduct, including, but not limited to the following: 1.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Deliberate fabrication of information, including falsification of data, credentials or other academically-related information.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Appropriation of research work done by others, including plagiarism – e.g.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: presenting methods, data or conclusions from a research project in which one has had no involvement, and direct copying of textual material – with intentional omission of acknowledgements.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: AA-4-1-1: ACADEMIC INTEGRITY IN RESEARCH AND PUBLICATION 3.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Use of data of a confidential nature, particularly that of a contracted research where secrecy may be necessary for a limited period, for one’s personal advantage or that of a third party.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Intentional or reckless violation of the principles of authorship.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Other conduct which seriously deviates from accepted ethical standards in scholarship.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Difference of interpretation or judgment, or honest error, do not constitute academic misconduct.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: STUDENT RESEARCH PLAGIARISM CHECK To guard against plagiarism, all student research reports will be subjected to electronic plagiarism checking before they are sent out to external examiners and before final defense is conducted.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: The Chair confirms in the Dissertation Dispatch Form that the dissertation has been subjected to electronic plagiarism checking and cleared.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Guidelines and Process for Final Plagiarism Check 1.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: The student submits the final research report – dissertation/thesis/research study/project/internship – to the electronic plagiarism checking system at least 14 days before the final examination.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: The system returns the Originality report to the IT Services Unit.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Within one week, the IT Services Unit reviews and analyzes the Originality report and sends the Originality report to the Chair of the student’s research committee.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: If the submitted paper is likely to contain plagiarized material, the IT Services Unit also sends a remark to the Chair.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: The Chair reviews the Originality report and informs the student.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: In case of plagiarism, the Chair requests the student to correct the report.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: The student makes the corrections and submits the corrected version to the Chair.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: The Chair approves the scheduling of the dissertation/thesis/research study/ research project final examination.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: The final examination will be delayed if the student can not submit the corrected version within the specified time.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: AA-4-1-1: ACADEMIC INTEGRITY IN RESEARCH AND PUBLICATION 9.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: The Chair confirms in the Record of Comprehensive Examination or Record of Thesis/Research Study/Project Examination that the report has been subjected to electronic plagiarism checking and cleared.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: Any attempt to circumvent the plagiarism check is considered a form of academic misconduct.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: DISCIPLINARY ACTION Any member of the AIT community who is aware of academic misconduct in research or publication may report the incident or practice to the School Dean or directly to the Vice President for Academic Affairs.
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: The procedures for disciplinary actions shall be according to the Institute Policy and Procedures on Employment of Faculty and of Staff: General Work Regulations and Student Handbook (section on Student Code of Conduct).
sentences.source: AA-4-1-1 Academic Integrity in Research and Publication rev18Mar2022.pdf

sentences.text: aitis ASIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY POLICY AND PROCEDURE STATEMENT Reference FB Issued By PRESIDENT VP-Administration / Section 6 Responsible Officer OFIN Director Financial and Serial No.
1-1 Date Issued 1 June 2005 Business Affairs No.
of Pages 9 Date Revised 8 June 2022 TITLE: CREDIT POLICY, FEES, AND INVOICING I.
PURPOSE To set forth the credit policy, fees, and invoicing and procedures of the Institute.

APPROVAL AUTHORITY Only in very exceptional circumstances is the President, or their VP designate, authorized to approve the delay or waiver of payments of registration, tuition, all other fees, and interest charges.
TUITION AND ALL OTHER FEES 1.

For self-support students and holders of externally-managed scholarships (externally-supported/sponsored students who have their own funds): (1) With regard to self-paying students and students with external sponsors, first semester/term fees must be paid in advance and/or fully paid upon enrolment, otherwise they will not be allowed to register.
Consequently, the student ID and Internet/e-mail access will not be released by Registry if fees are not paid.

(2) Continuing full-time postgraduate (PG) students (Master, Doctor and Diploma), i.e., those who completed at least one semester/term, will be invoiced for the applicable registration, tuition, and other fees each semester/term from the second semester/term onwards, which must be paid in advance and/or fully paid before each semester/term starts, otherwise they will not be allowed to register.
(3) For students under the Professional Master program, they will be invoiced for the applicable registration, tuition, and other fees for the second semester/term.
The invoice must be paid in advance and/or fully paid up before each semester/term starts, otherwise they will not be allowed to register.
FB-6-1-1: CREDIT POLICY (4) During the extended period, students will be invoiced for the registration fee and for the number of remaining credits.
The invoice must be paid in advance and/or fully paid up before each semester/term starts, otherwise they will not be allowed to register.

(5) Continuing part-time, including flexible program, students will be invoiced for the applicable registration, tuition, and other fees each semester/term from the second semester/term onwards, which must be paid in advance and/or fully paid before each semester/term starts, otherwise they will not be allowed to register.

Employee-students follow the stipulations on Payment of Fees in the P&P on Employee- Students on Part-Time Status (AA-8-8-2 IV.) (6) Registrations for each semester/term can only be activated upon full payment of tuition and all other fees.
(7) If tuition and other fees remain unpaid by the start of a semester/term, a student’s status shall be ‘suspended due to financial inability’.
Registration for the current semester/term and viewing of grades and transcripts will be disallowed.
(i) ‘Suspended’ students lose their status as students.

Thus, they cannot enroll in any course, nor can they avail of student privileges and benefits during the suspended period such as use of an AIT ID card, access to SIS and registration records, staying in AIT accommodation, use of Library and School facilities, discount of children in the AIT International School (AITIS), etc.
Their educational visas may also be cancelled.
Suspension status can only be lifted upon full payment of the outstanding fees and applicable interest charges before the deadline for adding/dropping of courses.
(8) Late payments, i.e., made after the start of the semester/term, but before the deadline for adding/dropping of courses, will be subject to an interest penalty of 1.5% per month.
(9) Online registration will no longer be allowed after the deadline for adding/dropping of courses.

(10) Additional payments for audited and repeated courses, as well as beyond the minimum 3 credits for part-time students, registered for the semester/term must be fully paid one week before the mid- semester/term examination period starts.

(11) If additional payments for audited and repeated courses remain unpaid a week prior to the mid-semester/term examination period, students with outstanding fees will be removed from the roster, and they will not be allowed to take any examinations.

FB-6-1-1: CREDIT POLICY (12) If fees remain unpaid after the deadline for adding/dropping of courses of the semester/term in which a student was ‘suspended due to financial inability’, the student shall be recommended for dismissal.
Once dismissed, Registry will notify the concerned units of the dismissal of the student.
(13) ‘Dismissed’ students lose their status as students.
Once dismissed, students who are staying in campus accommodation shall be asked to vacate their dormitory rooms; and their educational visas will be cancelled.
(14) ‘Dismissed’ students can no longer avail of student privileges and benefits after their dismissal.

Their student status can only be reinstated upon full payment of the applicable registration, tuition and other fees for the following semester/term, which must be paid in advance and/or fully paid before the semester/term starts.
(15) Students can be on ‘dismissed’ status for a maximum of two (2) semester or three (3) terms or one calendar year only.
A dismissed student due to financial inability could resume their studies up to the third semester or fourth term from the time of dismissal by paying the fees before the start of the semester/term.
Otherwise, reinstatement of student status will be possible only through re-application to AIT.
Upon readmission, the possibility to transfer all courses taken before will be governed by the policy of having taken the courses not more than five (5) years ago.
(16) The student cannot avail of the facility of non-payment one more time during the reinstated or re-admitted period.
Permanent dismissal will be applied to those who will be dismissed again due to non-payment of fees on time.
(17) Graduating students without financial clearance will not be allowed to take the final defense of their dissertation/thesis/research study/project/ internship.
(18) Graduating students with unpaid fees will not be permitted to graduate nor will they be allowed to rent gowns, and join the graduation rehearsal and ceremony.
They will also not be listed among the graduating students in the graduation booklet.
An interest penalty of 1.5% per month will apply on outstanding fees of graduating students.
(19) Provisional transcripts are not provided to self-support and externally- supported/sponsored students with any outstanding registration, tuition, and all other fees.
(20) Overdue accounts shall be reviewed periodically, and follow-up collections shall be initiated.
Sponsors must be invoiced for outstanding dues and requested to send AIT a promissory note detailing when and how payment will be made.
FB-6-1-1: CREDIT POLICY 2.

For holders of AIT-administered scholarships and those where AIT has active MoU/MoA/LoA (donor-funded students): (1) With regard to students who received AIT-administered scholarships and those who are covered by active MoU/MoA/LoA with AIT, credit is extended automatically.
(2) For these students, the interest penalty for delayed payments will not be applied.

(3) Students with donor funding who resign after the deadline for adding and dropping of courses will be liable to repay the total amount of tuition and registration fees that have been charged to the donor.

(4) Graduating students who are not able to complete financial clearance before graduation, due to outstanding financial obligations caused by the delay in the transmission of payment by their donors, are allowed to graduate and join the graduation ceremony if there is an endorsement from the Director of the concerned Offices based on the confirmation from the donor that payment will be transmitted to AIT soon.
(5) Transcripts and Degree Certificates of affected graduating students will be withheld by Registry.

Exception to this policy will only be made if the affected graduating student submits to Registry, through the concerned Office, proof or evidence that requires the Transcript or Degree Certificate to be presented to the external donor as part of the scholarship contractual obligations.
The Registry will provide the Transcript or Degree Certificate to the concerned Office to be forwarded to the external donor.
(6) The Office of Student Affairs (OSA) shall regularly follow up with external donor partners to ensure that payments are disbursed.
The OSA will monitor outstanding bills and reconcile with the Office of Finance (OFIN) on a regular basis, preferably twice a semester/term.
Postgraduate (PG) students are charged a registration fee per semester/term as a standard fee to maintain the status and privileges of an AIT student.
This also applies to students who are on research leave, sick leave, on exchange leave, and for those who have been extended in their study program.
Suspended and dismissed students, who lose their status as students, are not charged the registration fee.
There will be no refund of tuition and registration fees for all continuing students who resign after the deadline for adding and dropping of courses.
Provision on doubtful accounts will be applied on the outstanding invoices of inactive students and charged as expenses of the concerned Units.
FB-6-1-1: CREDIT POLICY E.
The delay or waiver of payments of registration, tuition, all other fees, and interest charges can only be approved by the President, or their VP designate.
Writing-off of doubtful accounts will be requested by OFIN and have to be approved by the President, or their VP designate.
ACCOMMODATION AND UTILITIES A.
Student Accommodation Rental (1) Students are required to pay a security deposit, which is collected in advance.
(2) Housing rental is payable on the first day of each month.
(3) With regard to unpaid rental and utilities, interest of 1.5% per month is imposed if the amount remains unpaid after 30 days.
(4) If the outstanding amount exceeds two-month rental, the students will be required to vacate the rooms.
(5) Eviction letters will be sent by OFAM/Accommodation Unit to the concerned students with a grace period to pay the outstanding amount, which include interest.
Otherwise, concerned students have to vacate the room within two weeks after the given deadline to pay.
(6) If accommodation and other fees remain unpaid beyond two months, a student’s status shall be ‘suspended/dismissed due to financial inability’.
Viewing of grades and transcripts will be disallowed.
(7) Allocation of new rooms to students (based on queuing) will not be allowed for those with outstanding accommodation rentals.
Employee Accommodation Rental (1) Employees are required to pay a two-month deposit, which is collected in advance.
(2) Invoices will be billed monthly and payment will be made through deduction from the employees’ monthly payroll account.
(3) In the case an employee has an insufficient amount available for deduction through the payroll account, an interest penalty of 1.5% per month will be imposed on the balance that remains unpaid.
(4) If a tenant defaults on any payment for a period of two successive months, the housing lease agreement may be terminated by written Page 5/9
FB-6-1-1: CREDIT POLICY notice from the Accommodation Unit, and the tenant is required to vacate the housing unit within two weeks.
The Office of Facilities and Asset Management (OFAM) will monitor outstanding bills and reconcile with OFIN on a quarterly basis.
The delay or waiver of payments of registration, tuition, all other fees, and interest charges can only be approved by the President, or their VP designate.
Writing-off of outstanding invoices, including interest due, will be requested by the OFAM Director and have to be approved by the President, or their VP designate, and forwarded to OFIN for action.
REQUEST FOR TRAVEL AUTHORIZATIONS AND PROMISSORY NOTES A.
All Requests for Travel Authorization (RTA) and Promissory Note (PN) by employees are required to be settled by the indicated due dates.
For RTAs, the settlement due date is a month after the date of arrival from official travel at AIT.
For PNs, the settlement due date is a month after the date committed by the requester.
If an RTA/PN has not been submitted for settlement to OFIN after the due date: (1) An interest penalty of 1.5% per month will be imposed.
(2) The outstanding amounts will be deducted from the employees’ payroll after an overdue period of two months.
Prior consent to do so is agreed to at the time of RTA and PN application.
(3) No new RTA/PN will be issued.

(4) The Office of Human Resources Services (OHRS) and OFIN will ensure that the renewal/reappointment of employment contracts will only be granted once all overdue RTA/PN is settled, and will confirm such to OHRS upon request for approval of renewal/reappointment.
CHILD SCHOOL FEES A.
AIT International School (AITIS) fees are charged according to the status of parents that will be verified at the beginning of each semester.
(1) For children of an AIT employee with a contract of employment of not less than one year, AIT subsidizes the full tuition fee by 75%.

(2) For children of AIT students of full-time degree programs, within their normal study period and with married accommodation units on the AIT campus duly approved by OSA, AIT discounts the AITIS fees by 50%.
FB-6-1-1: CREDIT POLICY AIT students of part-time degree programs and on extension are not entitled to discounts on AITIS fees for their children.
(3) Where both parents are entitled to receive assistance (discount/subsidy) with fees for the education of dependent children, the lower discount / subsidy rate applies.

(4) Where one parent is a seconded employee to AIT, concessionaire on the AIT campus, or are non-AIT employees, they are not eligible for any discount/subsidy of fees for the education of their children studying at AITIS.
With regard to parents (AIT students and employees) with more than one child: (1) Fees for the first child shall be paid in full within 30 days from the first day of the semester.
(1) Fees for the rest of the children shall be paid in full by the end of the second month of the semester.
With regard to other students, fees shall be paid in full within 30 days from the first day of the semester.
If the payment is not received within the periods stipulated, the AITIS students concerned shall be removed from the rolls of the school and shall not be allowed to attend classes.
AITIS will monitor outstanding invoices and reconcile with OFIN on a regular basis.
CONCESSIONAIRE RENTAL A.
Lessees are required to pay a three-month advance deposit to take on the lease of premises.
With regard to unpaid rental and utilities: (1) Interest penalty of 1.5% per month will be imposed on outstanding balances exceeding five days.
(2) If the outstanding amount exceeds two-month rental, lessees will be required to vacate the units.
OFAM will monitor outstanding invoices and reconcile with OFIN on a regular basis.
FB-6-1-1: CREDIT POLICY VIII.
AIT CONFERENCE CENTER A.
Dining (1) Personal meals are required to be settled.
No credit shall be granted.
(2) Meals that are charged to non-personal accounts must be authorized and payment is to be settled through internal charge.
In the event that there is insufficient budget, the person signing the invoice will be responsible for settlement.
Hotel/Conference Rooms (1) No credit is granted for individuals.

(2) With regard to third parties, a credit limit (not exceeding Baht-80,000) is to be evaluated and recommended on a case-by-case basis by the AIT Conference Center management based on credit standing and track record of the client and the limit established is to be reported to OFIN.
An interest penalty of 1.5% per month will be imposed for outstanding balance exceeding 30 days.
OFAM will monitor outstanding invoices and reconcile with OFIN on a regular basis.
SPONSORED CONTRACTS AND PROJECTS A.
Invoices are required to be settled by the indicated due date.
Overdue accounts shall be reviewed periodically and follow-up on collections shall be initiated.
The Sponsored & Contracted Projects Unit will monitor outstanding invoices.

sentences.text: of Pages 8 Date Revised 8 June 2022 TITLE: CAMPUS ACCOMMODATION FOR STUDENTS I.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: PURPOSE To present policies that define the eligibility and allocation procedures, regulations, sanctions on violations, and the rental charging system for on-campus student accommodation units.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: OPERATION AND MANAGEMENT The allocation of student accommodation is the responsibility of the Accommodation Unit of the Office of Facilities and Asset Management (OFAM).
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Any concerns involving student welfare that is related to accommodation should be brought to the attention of the Director of Student Affairs.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: DEFINITION AND SCOPE Student accommodation refers to a unit under the following dorm categories: Category 1: Dorms A, B, C, D, F, G, H Category 2: Dorms E, J, K Category 3: Dorms L, M, N, P3, Q, R, S, Category 4: Dorms T, U, V, W, X, Y Category 5: Dorms P1, P2, X11, Y11 (Married Units) Student Villages: I, II, III (Single and Married Units) IV.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students holding a current AIT student ID card and registered in the doctoral, master’s, certificate, or special program are provided with accommodation on campus by the Accommodation Unit.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Full-Time Doctoral and Master’s Degree Students.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Doctoral and master’s students are encouraged to live on campus for the first two semesters of their study period to experience the benefit of living in a multicultural community, Page 1/8
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: FS-1-1-1: CAMPUS ACCOMMODATION FOR STUDENTS which is part of the profile of an AIT graduate.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: For subsequent semesters, these students may opt to reside off-campus.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Non-Resident Doctoral Students are granted accommodation for a maximum of five months per year only.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Exceptions to this, on a case-to-case basis, are at the discretion of the OFAM Director.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Certificate Students are encouraged to stay on-campus for the entire study period.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Exceptions to this, on a case-to-case basis, may be given to those who have already exceeded their normal study period.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Married Students are permitted to bring their immediate families (spouse and children) to join them during their studies.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Married students and their immediate families can reside on-campus when they have been allocated a married accommodation unit.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Families of non-resident students may not reside on campus if the non-resident student is off-campus.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students with AIT-Employed Spouses.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Master’s or doctoral students with spouses who are AIT employees must register for staff accommodation.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students should move with the spouse when staff accommodation becomes available.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Failure of the student to move with their AIT-employed spouse will mean automatic eviction from the student dormitory.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students on Exchange/Dual-Degree Programs.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students, who will study in another country as part of the Exchange/Dual Degree program, are required to vacate their rooms while they study abroad.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Exceptions to this may only be given to those whose exchange program is less than two months.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Alumni/Employee/Interns.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: A student dormitory may be allocated for a maximum period of one month, in case of availability to: a.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: AIT employees upon evidence of employee ID c.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Student Interns (from partner universities/organizations) 8.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: All guests must be registered with the Accommodation Unit three days before arrival.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: With prior approval of the Accommodation Unit, guests may stay in student dormitories for a maximum period of one week per semester.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: In the case of overstaying guests, special prior permission must be obtained from the OFAM Director and a daily rent will be charged for the room.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: To maintain a decent living environment, it is recommended to restrict the number of residents to two per room in a single unit or two per bedroom in a married unit.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Extra provisions (such as mattresses, etc.) for the guests may be requested from the Accommodation Unit, if available and upon payment of a nominal service charge.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: The Institute shall in no way be held responsible for the legal status of guests.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Visas, residence permits, etc.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: shall be the responsibility of the guests.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: FS-1-1-1: CAMPUS ACCOMMODATION FOR STUDENTS 9.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: All requests to reside off-campus will require the approval of the concerned Advisor and the Student Affairs Director.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Temporary Stay in Employee Accommodation.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students may put their names on the waiting list and live in employee accommodation subject to availability.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Please note however that the first priority will always be for employees in the queue.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: If there are no employees in the queue then the available employee accommodation will be offered to the student on the top of the waiting list.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students are allowed to temporarily stay in such units until such time that they have been allocated a room in student accommodation or until advised by the Accommodation Unit.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Queuing and temporary stay in employee accommodation requires prior approval from the OFAM Director.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Graduating/Completing Students.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students are required to vacate their rooms within five days (including weekends) after their graduation/completion of studies.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Extended stay requires the approval of the OFAM Director.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students who are funded by scholarships will not be entitled to an accommodation rent refund.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: ALLOCATION PROCEDURES 1.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Allocation of Rooms to New Students.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: It is recommended that new students be on campus during the arrival period specified in the admission offer letter.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: ● New students are served in a queue based on a ‘take a number’ system drawn by lot (i.e., students stand in line and in this order select a random number from the box; this number becomes the queuing number and, in this sequence, students wait their turn to select an accommodation unit).
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: ● New students may choose from all the available units when their queuing number is called.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Waiting List System.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: New and continuing students wishing to move into another category of rooms may queue during the period designated by the Accommodation Unit.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Queuing requires presentation of a student ID.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: ● A student may queue for a maximum of three dorm categories in order of preference.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: ● By queuing in more than one category the student risks being allocated the least preferred room.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: ● A student may put special requirements in the "specifications" column of the waiting list.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: ● A student residing on campus must put their name on the waiting list in person, at the Accommodation Unit.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: FS-1-1-1: CAMPUS ACCOMMODATION FOR STUDENTS ● Only continuing students, who are off-campus on data collection or on approved leave, may put their names on the waiting list through email to accommodation@ait.asia.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: They must provide the approximate date of their arrival on campus.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: ● A student wishing to move within the same dorm category requires prior approval of the OFAM Director.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Allocation According to Waiting List ● An offer for transfer/relocation from one unit to another according to the waiting list shall start during the period designated by the Accommodation Unit and is done every Friday.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: ● Allocation based on the waiting list is done only during the semester.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: The Accommodation Unit will inform concerned students on unit allocation specifics (e.g.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: new room number, dates of transfer, etc.) by email.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: ● A student shall have five working days to accept the offer.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Failure to accept the allocated unit shall result in the removal of the student from the waiting list.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: ● The allocation to continuing students on the waiting list will be suspended during the arrival of new students, up until the last day of adding/dropping.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: ● Category 2 dorms (E, J, K) are allocated to continuing students only.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: These dorms are continually allocated, as vacancies arise.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Allocation of Student Villages.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: New and continuing students may apply for student villages following the policies below: Unmarried Students in Shared Unit ● Students may apply for a shared unit provided they are able to find a group of three students with similar entry and graduation dates, who mutually agree to live together.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Student groupings will then be included on the waiting list for the allocation of shared units.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: A changed grouping after the application has been made will necessitate lodging of a new application.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: A change in the grouping after allocation is not permitted unless otherwise approved by the OFAM Director.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: ● If shared units become available, the first group on the waiting list will be notified.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: The students concerned will be given five working days to confirm the offer to move; otherwise, the group may lose the offer.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Married Students ● Married applicants will be asked if and when they wish to bring their families to AIT, and informed that there are limited married accommodation units.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: If married accommodation is not available, new students may be given the option to reside with their family off-campus (provided that prior approval from the Advisor and Student Affairs Director has been granted), or in AIT employee accommodation units, if available.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: In such instances, the Accommodation Unit will provide information on the types, locations, and rents of suitable accommodation units.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: FS-1-1-1: CAMPUS ACCOMMODATION FOR STUDENTS ● Married students choosing to remain unaccompanied for some or all of their study period at AIT will be subject to the same campus accommodation policies and procedures as unmarried students above for as long as they remain unaccompanied.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students are responsible for keeping their accommodation units, furniture, and equipment clean and in good order.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: No furniture or equipment can be removed from the accommodation unit or transferred from one dormitory to another without the prior approval of the Accommodation Unit.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Student dormitories are inventoried and checked regularly.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students are responsible to clean the vacated room before they transfer to another unit on campus, otherwise they will be subject to a cleaning fee.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Every accommodation unit is maintained as the need arises and, once vacated, will be renovated according to Institute standards.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Furniture and fittings will be changed as necessary, due to wear and tear.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: All maintenance queries must be reported to the Call Center on 02 524 5800 between 0800 and 1700 hours, Monday to Saturday.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Only emergency work will be carried out beyond these times.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Internal cleaning is not carried out, while deep cleaning is performed every 4 months.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: External cleaning and ground maintenance are carried out as necessary.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Amenities like garbage bags and tissue rolls are not provided.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Regular cleaning and hygiene of the unit has to be maintained by the student.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: OFAM is authorized to undertake random inspection and may lay sanctions if the unit is found to be dirty and unhygienic.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students have to upkeep the cleanliness of the common area and landscape around the building.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: No pot plants are allowed on the parapet, staircase or other areas around the units that cause disturbance.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Linen (one set) is provided to each student living in accommodation units irrespective of the type of unit.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: One set of linen (one pillowcase and two bedsheets) and one pillow are provided in accommodation units to new students.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: These are the property of the student, to be used and maintained/washed by the student for the duration of their student status on campus.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: This linen set and pillow may be kept when leaving AIT.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Additional linen is available to purchase through AU.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Cooking is not permitted, except in accommodation units where cooking facilities or shared cooking facilities are provided.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: FS-1-1-1: CAMPUS ACCOMMODATION FOR STUDENTS 10.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: The use of flammable/combustible cooking appliances, like LPG, is permitted only in accommodation units with cooking facilities.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students are allowed to install outdoor antennas or cable TV, only with the prior agreement of the Accommodation Unit.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Split connections are strictly prohibited.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students are not allowed to install heavy appliances, such as air-conditioners, in accommodation units that are not designed for such facilities, i.e.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: in Student Villages and Category 1 dorms.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Upon leaving the accommodation unit, students are required to leave their units clean, remove all possessions, and to leave the unit and its furniture and equipment in the same condition as when the tenancy began.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: For graduating students, the advance security deposit charged to each student at the time of first allocation of an accommodation unit on campus, will be used if necessary, to pay for any service, loss, or damage to accommodation units, or for other debts incurred during occupation.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students shall not disturb fellow students in the residential areas.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Games and activities shall cease at 2300 hours at all sports fields and courts in and near campus residential areas.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Noisy group studies and parties are not allowed in student accommodation.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Alternative rooms designated for such purpose must be used.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students who misplace their accommodation unit keys may ask the Accommodation Unit to open their dorms.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: If this service is required after office hours, i.e.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: 1630 hours onwards, a minimal fee will be required and Security at the main entrance to AIT will need to be contacted.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: To permit the Accommodation Unit to enter the said premises for inspection at all reasonable times.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: SANCTIONS Infringements of any of the above regulations will mean sanctions against all parties involved.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: The sanctions may include withdrawal of names from waiting lists, community service, formal reprimand, fine, suspension, or ban from campus accommodation.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Graduating students residing beyond the five day limit (including weekends) after graduation, without prior approval from the OFAM Director may have their accommodation unit sealed by the Accommodation Unit.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Furniture and appliances should not be removed from the accommodation unit.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: The fine for removal of furniture and appliances is Baht 500 per item.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Repair and replacement of furniture and appliances must be requested from and Page 6/8
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: FS-1-1-1: CAMPUS ACCOMMODATION FOR STUDENTS approved by the Accommodation Unit, which may ask the tenant to pay additional charges, in situations where damage to furniture and appliances has resulted from direct negligence by the tenant.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students who cook in dormitories where cooking is prohibited may face a minimum fine of Baht 2,000, or the actual costs of the damage caused by cooking.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students who install heavy appliances, such as air-conditioners, in accommodation units not designed for such facilities, will be fined Baht 2,000 and charged for the services related to the removal of the appliances.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Smoking, celebratory burning, or bonfires in and near student accommodation is strictly prohibited.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students found guilty may face a fine of Baht 2,000, or the actual costs of the damage caused by smoking or burning.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Pets are not allowed in student accommodation.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: The fine for non-compliance is Baht 2,000.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students disturbing the peace of their surroundings shall receive a formal reprimand as a first warning.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Repeated disturbances may face further sanctions.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Rooms must be used for residential purposes only.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Sub-letting rooms may result in eviction from student accommodation.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Students who do not maintain the upkeep and cleanliness of their bedroom, bathroom, corridor, balcony, and cooking facilities, where provided, will be fined Baht 2,000 and charged for cleaning services of the unit.
sentences.source: FS-1-1-1 Campus Accommodation for Students AMT8Jun2022.pdf

sentences.text: Special and exchange students are required to pay the rent in full for the whole duration of their stay on campus.
